summary: Closed, unmerged PR attempting to match stage function grInishie1_801FA9B4. The PR body says the function reached 100% by copying the _StageData structure pattern from grHeal. The diff primarily adds grInishie1 stage data/storage and adjusts grInishie1_801FA9B4 to read OSReport strings through that struct; it also includes unrelated-looking codegen steering edits in grYorster and lbSnap. An author comment says earlier commits were pushed and the PR was closed to submit only the stage data fix.

key_files.role: Primary target file for grInishie1_801FA9B4; adds stage-data-related symbols, a local grInishie1_StageData wrapper, OSReport string storage, and a callback pointer expression based on nearby data layout.
Secondary touched stage file with loop and cast/codegen-shaping edits in grYorster_80202428; author comment suggests this may have been part of earlier commits rather than final intended scope.
Secondary touched library file with local pointer and offset temporary changes in lbSnap_8001DF20; author comment suggests this may have been part of earlier commits rather than final intended scope.

what_changed.text: Added grI1_803E48C8 as an s16 array and used it as StageData collision or block data via a cast to S16Vec3*.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:grinishie1.c added grI1_803E48C8 and StageData initializer

what_changed.text: Added grI1_803E4944 as the string "/GrI1.dat", grI1_803E49A8 as "grinishie1.c", and grI1_803E4950 as a grInishie1_StageData object containing StageData plus an OSReport format string field.

Changed grInishie1_801FA9B4 from direct callback-table indexing to a casted pointer expression derived from grI1_803E48C8 + 0x2c, then moved gobj initialization after the callback pointer declaration.
Changed the missing-gobj OSReport call to load the format and filename through grI1_803E4950.report_fmt_get_gobj and grI1_803E49A8 instead of inline literals.
Introduced a local HSD_JObj* jobj2 before DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP in grInishie1_801FBC4C.
Changed grYorster_80202428 to use a manual-increment for loop, a temporary s32 loaded from an element pointer field, u32 casts for comparison, and a temporary f32 newValue before assignment.
Changed lbSnap_8001DF20 to introduce descPtr and offset locals, then access lbSnap_803BACC8 through descPtr.

smart_moves.text: The author used an existing matched analogue, grHeal, as evidence for the _StageData structure shape needed to match grInishie1_801FA9B4.
The author recognized the PR had extra earlier commits and closed it to resubmit a narrower stage-data fix.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Matched sibling stage files can be useful evidence for recovering stage-data wrapper shapes, but this PR only supports that as a grInishie1/grHeal candidate pattern, not as a global rule.

When a code match depends on adjacent data layout, the diff may expose pointer casts from one data symbol into the next table, as in the callback pointer expression based on grI1_803E48C8 + 0x2c. This should be treated as a matching clue requiring data-ownership review rather than automatically accepted style.
Scope control matters: a PR targeting one stage function became mixed with grYorster and lbSnap edits, and the author closed it to submit just the stage-data fix.

naming_conventions.text: New grInishie1 data symbols in the diff use the local grI1_ address-style prefix, such as grI1_803E48C8, grI1_803E4944, grI1_803E4950, and grI1_803E49A8.
The new local wrapper type is named grInishie1_StageData and embeds StageData as stage_data plus a named report_fmt_get_gobj char array.
Secondary edits introduce descriptive locals descPtr, offset, jobj2, temp, and newValue; these are observed names only, not evidence of accepted naming practice.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: StageData layout tactic: add a local struct containing StageData followed by a report-format string field so later code can reference OSReport text through the same object.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Current standards caution against promoting literals into storage without data ownership evidence; this PR is closed and unmerged.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Data-adjacency tactic: compute a StageCallbacks* using a cast from grI1_803E48C8 plus 0x2c instead of direct grI1_803E48F4 indexing.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Useful as evidence of layout pressure, but raw pointer arithmetic should be reviewed against typed-field and data-ownership standards.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Local lifetime/register tactic: assign gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2 to a local HSD_JObj* before invoking DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Narrow codegen tactic; no objdiff output is included in the slice.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Loop/codegen tactic in grYorster_80202428: manual loop increment, pointer-field load through s32 temp, explicit u32 comparison casts, and f32 assignment temporary.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Author comment implies this may be outside the intended final PR scope.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Alias/local-order tactic in lbSnap_8001DF20: introduce descPtr and offset locals before writing fields and passing descPtr to lb_8001C4A8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Author comment implies this may be outside the intended final PR scope.

review_feedback.text: No review comments or review records are present in the loaded slice.
Author issue comment: "Pushed some earlier commits, closing to submit just the stage data fix".

follow_up_queries: Search for later PR that resubmitted only the grInishie1 stage data fix after PR #2557.
Search grHeal stage data wrapper patterns to compare with grInishie1_StageData.
Search current src/melee/gr/grinishie1.c for grI1_803E4950 and grInishie1_801FA9B4 final accepted form.
Search for PRs touching grYorster_80202428 around manual increment or u32 cast loop matching.
Search for PRs touching lbSnap_8001DF20 descPtr offset local matching.

notes: Diff and PR text are available and untruncated for the key facts.
The PR is closed with merged_at null, so no acceptance or final source state should be inferred.
No objdiff, build, checkdiff, review approval, or reviewer feedback is included.

The author explicitly says earlier commits were pushed and the PR was closed to submit just the stage data fix, making the grYorster and lbSnap hunks likely out-of-scope for the intended final change, but the slice does not identify which exact commits were intended to remain.
The PR body claims 100% match for grInishie1_801FA9B4; this is author-provided evidence only and not independently validated in the slice.

source_update_candidates.title: Possible grInishie1 stage-data wrapper analogue from grHeal
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2557 says grInishie1_801FA9B4 matched to 100% by copying the same _StageData structure found in grHeal, and the diff adds a grInishie1_StageData wrapper containing StageData plus report_fmt_get_gobj. Because the PR was closed unmerged, treat this only as a candidate historical clue for grInishie1/grHeal data-layout comparison.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-specific data-layout clue, but not accepted source fact due to closed unmerged status and missing validation.

source_update_candidates.title: Keep mixed-scope cleanup separate from focused matching PRs
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2557 targeted grInishie1_801FA9B4 but also included grYorster and lbSnap codegen edits; the author closed it after noting earlier commits were pushed and planned to submit just the stage data fix. Curator may consider whether existing standards should more explicitly discourage bundling unrelated matching tactics with a focused function/data-layout match.
source_update_candidates.reason: Supported by PR history, but should be curated as a standard update only if not already covered elsewhere.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the grInishie1_StageData layout as accepted current source fact from this slice alone; the PR was closed unmerged.
Do not treat OSReport string-to-struct storage as an accepted cleanup pattern; current standards caution against literal/data ownership changes without evidence.
Do not treat the grYorster manual loop/cast edits or lbSnap descPtr edits as endorsed tactics; the author comment indicates they may have been accidental earlier commits and no validation is present.
Do not infer reviewer intent or acceptance; no review comments or reviews are loaded.
